Jeremiah Hackett

Clinical Social Worker

I work with college students and young professionals. My clients are typically concerned about their relationships, behaviors, thoughts and moods.

For some, talking can really help.

Being compassionately interviewed gives us a chance to speak without hesitation or fear of recrimination. There aren't many spaces where we're allowed to be fully (and safely) candid with ourselves. This timeless process disrupts rigid and unhelpful ways of thinking. It facilitates a more humane (and humanizing) view of ones self — and others.

I offer psychotherapy services in Maine and New York. I contract with Headway to accept these insurances.

I also offer clinical consulting services (or 'coaching') to executives, creatives, and developers. Explore below to see how these services differ.

Curious if we might be a good fit? You can schedule a consultation, or drop me an email if you have any questions.

Common Questions
What insurances do you accept?

I contract with Headway to accept a variety of insurances:

  • Aetna
  • Anthem BC/BS
  • Cigna
  • Oscar
  • Oxford
  • Point32 Healthcare
  • United Healthcare

To verify your coverage and learn how Headway works, click here.

How do coaching and psychotherapy differ?

Learn More
About Me

I grew up in rural Maine in the 1980s, balancing long bike rides and my Nintendo. I studied writing and philosophy as an undergraduate, and taught high school English and history.

I earned my master’s in social work in 2012 and did clinical work in communities and residential settings in addition to supervising case managers. I entered private practice ten years ago.

Four years ago, I decided to specialize in the psychological development and sociocultural demands of men.

I still bike and game, though my tastes have evolved. I’m currently playing Hades 2.
